author: Clement of Rome
book: The First Epistle of Clement to the Corinthians.

classification.cautionReason: Valued early Christian text, but not part of the biblical canon for mainstream Christian denominations.

lineage: The First Epistle of Clement to the Corinthians.
God Sees All Things: Therefore Let Us Avoid Transgression.

ccel.title: ANF09. The Gospel of Peter, The Diatessaron of Tatian, The Apocalypse of Peter, the Vision of Paul, The Apocalypse of the Virgin and Sedrach, The Testament of Abraham, The Acts of Xanthippe and Polyxena, The Narrative of Zosimus, The Apology of Aristides, The Epistles of Clement (complete text), Origen’s Commentary on John, Books 1–10, and Commentary on Matthew, Books 1, 2, and 10–14.

verses.book: The First Epistle of Clement to the Corinthians.
verses.text: Since then all things are seen and heard [by God], let us fear Him, and forsake those wicked works which proceed from evil desires; so that, through His mercy, we may be protected from the judgments to come.

verses.book: The First Epistle of Clement to the Corinthians.
verses.text: For whither can any of us flee from His mighty hand?

verses.book: The First Epistle of Clement to the Corinthians.
verses.text: Or what world will receive any of those who run away from Him?

verses.book: The First Epistle of Clement to the Corinthians.
verses.text: For the Scripture saith in a certain place, “Whither shall I go, and where shall I be hid from Thy presence?

verses.book: The First Epistle of Clement to the Corinthians.
verses.text: If I ascend into heaven, Thou art there; if I go away even to the uttermost parts of the earth, there is Thy right hand; if I make my bed in the abyss, there is Thy Spirit.”

verses.book: The First Epistle of Clement to the Corinthians.
verses.text: Whither, then, shall anyone go, or where shall he escape from Him who comprehends all things?
